name: Salary Calculator
metaTitle: Salary Calculator — In-Hand Salary from CTC | c7xai
metaDescription: Calculate your monthly and annual in-hand salary from CTC. See full salary breakup — basic, HRA, PF, tax — for both old and new tax regimes. Client-side, zero PII.

faq.question: How is in-hand salary calculated from CTC?
faq.answer: CTC (Cost to Company) includes your gross salary plus employer contributions like EPF. In-hand salary = Gross Salary - Employee PF - Professional Tax - Income Tax. Gross Salary = CTC - Employer PF. Your monthly in-hand is what actually lands in your bank account.

faq.question: What is the difference between CTC and in-hand salary?
faq.answer: CTC is the total cost a company bears for you, including employer PF contributions and variable pay. In-hand salary is your take-home pay after all deductions — employee PF (12% of basic), professional tax (₹200/month), and income tax. Typically, in-hand salary is 70-80% of CTC.

faq.question: Should I choose the old or new tax regime?
faq.answer: The new regime (FY 2025-26) offers lower slab rates but fewer deductions — only standard deduction of ₹75,000 and employer NPS under 80CCD(2). The old regime allows 80C (₹1.5L), 80D, HRA exemption, and more. If your total deductions exceed ~₹4.25L, the old regime usually saves more tax.

faq.question: How much PF is deducted from my salary?
faq.answer: Employee PF contribution is 12% of basic salary (same as employer contribution). For example, if your basic is ₹50,000/month, your PF deduction is ₹6,000/month. EPF wage ceiling is ₹15,000/month basic for mandatory coverage, but most IT companies calculate on actual basic.
